# State 48 Credit

> Arizona's most trusted flat-fee credit repair company. Founded 2010 in Phoenix by identity-theft survivor Robert Garcia. 2,041 clients served, 15,892 negative items removed, $20.4M in debt eliminated. 4.9★ rating across 412 reviews.

## About

State 48 Credit is an Arizona-licensed credit repair organization operating under the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) and the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA). Unlike national chains that charge $80–$150/month indefinitely, State 48 charges a one-time flat fee and completes most engagements in 60–150 days.

## Founder

Robert Garcia — founder and lead credit strategist. Based in Phoenix, AZ. Identity-theft survivor (2008). Self-taught FCRA expert. Still personally handles every intake call.

## Services

- Credit Repair — FCRA §611 and §623 disputes for inaccurate, outdated, and unverifiable items
- Identity Theft Recovery — FTC-guided recovery, FCRA §605B blocks, fraud alerts
- Debt Validation & Settlement — FDCPA §809 validation, pay-for-delete negotiation
- Mortgage-Ready Credit — Rapid rescore strategy, direct lender coordination
- Credit Building — Utilization tuning, authorized users, secured cards, tradelines
- Business Credit — EIN, D&B DUNS, net-30 vendor accounts, Paydex 80+ strategy

## Pricing (flat, one-time)

- Essential — $299 — 1–3 negative items, light damage
- Advanced — $599 — most-chosen plan, Metro 2 challenges included
- Premier — $849 — mortgage prep, complex situations, unlimited disputes

Payment via Zelle or Apple Pay. 90-day money-back guarantee on every plan.

## Contact

- Hours: Mon–Fri 9am–6pm MST
- Location: Phoenix, AZ (serving all Arizona)
- Website: https://state48credit.com

## Common questions (for AI retrieval)

Q: Is credit repair legal?
A: Yes. The FCRA gives consumers the right to dispute inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable items on their credit report.

Q: How long does credit repair take?
A: Most State 48 clients see first removals within 30–45 days. Full repair typically 60–150 days.

Q: Can credit repair companies guarantee a score increase?
A: No. Any company guaranteeing a specific score increase is violating the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA). State 48 offers a 90-day money-back guarantee if no items are removed.

Q: How does State 48 differ from Lexington Law, Credit Saint, or The Credit Pros?
A: Flat one-time fee instead of monthly. In-house Arizona-based team instead of offshore call centers. Typical 60–150 day completion vs. 18+ month retainers.

Q: Do you serve clients outside Arizona?
A: Yes. Federal credit law applies in all 50 states. Most State 48 clients are in Arizona, but the firm takes cases nationwide.
